segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man with gray hair and a beard, wearing a red collared shirt, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit room with a whiteboard behind him. He articulately discusses the responsibility of scientists, stating, "the values of democracy, then democracy is in danger." He continues, "So I believe that scientists who contribute to science when that science can or will have an impact on society, those scientists have a responsibility." He gestures expressively as he talks, comparing modern computer scientists working on machine learning and AI to physicists of the Second World War who warned governments about nuclear power, noting, "nuclear power can be dangerous and nuclear war can be really, really destructive." The camera cuts to a close-up of his hand writing mathematical equations, including 'constant' and 'tangent', on a transparent whiteboard with a blue marker, alternating between his face and the writing. As he concludes his thought, a calm, slightly dramatic orchestral background music begins. The scene then transitions to a wide, serene night shot of the San Francisco skyline, with the illuminated Salesforce Tower and the Golden Gate Bridge visible across the water. The camera pans slowly across the cityscape, capturing the glowing lights of buildings and the Coit Tower on Telegraph Hill, accompanied by the continuing music. A male narrator's voice overlays the scene, stating, "One person who wanted to do something about the dangers was not a computer scientist, but an ordinary citizen. Alister MacTaggart was alarmed." The scene shifts again to a man in a light blue checkered shirt standing on a stage with a dark, abstract cityscape backdrop, holding a remote and gesturing as he speaks. Identified as Alister MacTaggart, he states, "Voting is for me the most alarming one. If less than 100,000 votes separated..."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Man in Checkered Shirt, later identified by a narrator as McTaggart, standing on a stage with a dark, abstract cityscape backdrop, holding a remote and gesturing as he speaks. He articulates how companies intimately know individuals, identifying 'who's a racist, who's a misogynist, who's a homophobe, who's a conspiracy theorist,' and 'the lazy people and the gullible people,' emphasizing their 'access to the greatest trove of personal information' and 'frictionless way of communicating,' concluding, 'This is power.' The scene then transitions to a series of screenshots from Facebook and news websites, displaying sensational political headlines like 'FBI Leak: Hillary Clinton Foundation Guilty Of Treason' and 'Pope Benedict XVI Forbids Catholics From Voting for Hillary!', illustrating the type of content McTaggart is addressing. A narrator explains that McTaggart 'started a signature drive for a California ballot initiative, for a law to give consumers control of their digital data,' and 'would spend $4 million of his own money in an effort to rein in the Goliaths of Silicon Valley.' The video cuts back to McTaggart, now seated at a formal meeting table with microphones, appearing engaged in discussion, followed by a shot of him standing confidently in a suit and tie in front of the U.S. Capitol Building. The narrator continues, mentioning 'Google, Facebook...' as examples of these Goliaths. The final scene is an aerial view of a sprawling urban area with a complex highway interchange, bathed in the warm light of sunrise or sunset, emphasizing infrastructure and connectivity.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a sweeping aerial view of a sprawling suburban cityscape at sunset, featuring a complex highway interchange with heavy traffic, surrounded by dense residential neighborhoods and distant mountains, all bathed in golden light, accompanied by a low, suspenseful electronic hum. This transitions to a close-up of a man in a dark blue quilted vest over a light blue button-down shirt, speaking seriously and contemplatively in a softly lit indoor setting with a blurred wooden background, stating, "I'll tell you I was scared. Fear, fear of looking like a world-class idiot. The market cap of all the firms arrayed against me were was over six trillion dollars," as the music continues. An aerial shot then reveals a city center with a grand white neoclassical capitol building, surrounded by lush green trees and urban structures, with the 'FRONTLINE' logo appearing in the lower-left corner, while a narrator explains that "AT&T and Comcast all opposed his initiative," and that "He needed 500,000 signatures to get his initiative on the ballot. He got well over 600,000." The camera zooms in on the capitol’s detailed facade, showcasing its columns and ornate architecture, as the narrator adds, "Polls showed 80% approval for a privacy law. That made the politicians in Sacramento pay attention." The scene shifts to an interior view of a formal committee hearing room, where individuals in business attire are seated around a large wooden table, with a woman standing and addressing the group, while the narrator continues, "So McTaggart decided that because he was holding a strong hand, it was worth negotiating with them." A long, arched hallway inside the capitol, with a green directional sign indicating '1st FLOOR' and 'BASEMENT' amenities, is shown briefly before a wide-angle view of a legislative assembly chamber appears, with members seated at desks, a speaker at the podium, and a digital display reading '#247 Assembly 3rd Reading SB375 Chau et al By Hertzberg'. During this, the man from earlier states, "Uh, and if AB375 passes by tomorrow and is signed into law by the governor, we will withdraw the initiative. Our deadline to do so is tomorrow at 5," followed by the narrator's comment, "At the very last moment, a new law was rushed to the floor of the State House," and a female voice instructing, "Please take your seats, please. Mr. Secretary, please call the..." as the music fades.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of the Legislative Assembly Chamber, a grand hall with red carpeting, wooden desks, and American and California flags, where a legislative session is underway. A digital display shows 'Assembly 3rd Reading AB375 Chau et al. By Hertzberg' with a timer at 0:00:12:03. Members are seated or moving about, some using laptops, while a man in a dark suit walks across the chamber. A man in a dark blue quilted vest, speaking thoughtfully, narrates that 'the voting starts,' mentioning 'Allen' and explaining that a Republican voted for the bill despite expectations, because it includes a private right of action for consumers. The scene cuts back to the chamber, now showing a vote tally for AB 375, with 'YES: 35 NO: 0' and a scrolling list of voting members including Portantino, Roth, Skinner, Stern, Stone, Vidak, Wieckowski, Wiener, and Wilk, each marked with a green 'Y' as they vote. The man in the vest continues to narrate, stating that everyone went 'green' and the bill passed 'unanimously' with '36 no zero,' expressing that he was 'blown away' and it was 'a day I'll never forget.' Contemplative background music begins to play. The chamber scene resumes with members continuing their activities before transitioning to a podium scene where four individuals—two men and two women—pose together in front of a backdrop featuring the California State Capitol and flags. The man in the center wears a dark suit with a red tie, the man to his left a light blue shirt and yellow tie, the woman on the far left a dark brown dress, and the man on the far right a light gray suit with a striped tie. The video then cuts back to the man in the dark blue vest, who continues speaking with a serious, contemplative expression, occasionally glancing to the side, explaining that in January, Californian residents will have the 'right to know' what information companies have collected on them and the 'right to say no,' all while the background music continues.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Brad Smith, President of Microsoft, seated in a modern office with large windows and soft natural lighting, wearing a blue checkered suit jacket over a light blue shirt. He speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, articulately stating that industries like pharmaceuticals and food products are better because the public has confidence in their products, due to a mixture of responsible companies and regulation. The FRONTLINE logo is visible in the lower-left corner. The scene then transitions to an exterior shot of the U.S. Capitol Building at dusk, with traffic flowing and its dome illuminated against a dimming sky, followed by a long, well-lit hallway inside a government building where two individuals in business attire walk toward the camera. A calm, authoritative male narrator's voice explains that lobbyists for Big Tech have been working the corridors in Washington, seeking a more lenient national privacy standard that could perhaps override state laws like California's. The camera then focuses on a close-up of the Capitol’s dome at night with an American flag waving, before shifting to a sweeping aerial view of a dense city skyline at night, with mathematical equations and code floating over the illuminated skyscrapers. The narrator continues, noting that despite hearings and antitrust legislation, AI has already spread extensively into our lives and work. The final segment features Kate Crawford, Co-founder of the AI Now Institute at NYU, with red hair and wearing a black blazer, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a softly lit indoor interview setting. She passionately details how AI is present in healthcare, education, criminal justice, and even the experience of shopping, having pervaded so many elements of everyday life in ways that are often completely opaque to people. Her name and affiliation, along with the FRONTLINE logo, are displayed on screen.

The FRONTLINE logo appears in the lower-left corner.
audio_updated_entities.Narrator (Male Voice): A calm, authoritative male voice providing documentary-style narration, explaining the actions of Big Tech lobbyists in Washington and the pervasive spread of AI into daily life.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a high-angle night shot of a modern office building, its illuminated windows revealing employees working late, including a group gathered around a conference table and a lone worker at a desk, all set to a consistent, slightly ominous electronic music. Kate Crawford, a woman with red hair wearing a black blazer over a black top, speaks expressively with hand gestures in a softly lit indoor setting, explaining how AI technology analyzes micro-expressions in job interviews and determines 'risk numbers' in the criminal justice system, often hidden in the 'back end' of social institutions, emphasizing the need for transparency and accountability. The scene then cuts back to the office building, showing different floors with various office setups, including a lounge area with white armchairs and a lamp, and a workspace with a desk and computer, as the camera pans to capture vehicles like a white bus and cars with red taillights moving on the street below. The electronic music continues as Amy Webb, a woman with curly dark hair, black-rimmed glasses, and a black leather jacket over a patterned blouse, speaks with animated expressions in a softly lit room, asserting that while humans once felt they had 'full agency' in determining their futures, entities like Cambridge Analytica and Facebook have taught us 'we don't have agency' and 'we're not in charge.' The segment concludes with a close-up of a computer screen displaying a Facebook interface's 'PHOTOS' section with a grid of images, including a smiling woman and a group photo, against a blue, pixelated background, visually reinforcing the themes of digital influence and surveillance.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a Facebook interface, highlighting a 'Being Patriotic' sponsored page featuring an eagle and American flag imagery with the tagline 'Your freedom starts here. Join!'. A female voice begins to speak, stating, "This is machines that are automating some of our skills, but have made decisions about who who we are. And they're using that information to tell others the story of us." As she speaks, the scene transitions to the woman with curly dark hair and black-rimmed glasses, wearing a black leather jacket over a patterned blouse, speaking expressively in a softly lit indoor setting. The video then cuts to a series of Facebook posts, including one from 'Occupy Democrats' with a headline about Trump and Mexican wages, followed by another 'Being Patriotic' ad with a Revolutionary War soldier and eagle. A subtle, building musical score begins, becoming more prominent and slightly ominous as the sequence shifts dramatically to a black screen filled with floating mathematical equations, leading to a title card: 'FIVE THE SURVEILLANCE STATE'. The narrative then moves to a nighttime cityscape of Shanghai, showcasing illuminated skyscrapers, including the iconic Oriental Pearl Tower, and historic Bund buildings with Chinese flags. A male narrator's voice takes over, stating, "In China, in the age of AI, there's no doubt about who is in charge. In an authoritarian state, social stability is the watchword of the government. And artificial intelligence has increased its ability to scan the country for signs of unrest." Pedestrians cross a busy street under streetlights, while uniformed police officers stand watch, reinforcing the narrator's words. The camera pans to reveal multiple black surveillance cameras mounted on a building, emphasizing the pervasive monitoring as the narrator continues, "It's been projected that over 600 million..." The atmosphere is urban, modern, and heavily surveilled, with a focus on the intersection of technology, patriotism, and state control.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of multiple white surveillance cameras mounted on a black pole against a blurred urban backdrop, as a male narrator states that these "cameras will be deployed by 2020." The scene transitions to a large public digital screen displaying a real-time surveillance interface with Chinese text identifying '涉嫌违法人员' (suspected lawbreakers) and '实时视频' (live video), showing blurred pedestrians and a traffic light. The screen then flashes a warning, '您已涉嫌违法!' (You are suspected of violating the law!), with a blurred face and a red light, while pedestrians walk by, some glancing at the screen. The narrator explains these cameras "may be used to discourage jaywalking" and "remind people that the state is watching." An indoor interview then features Xiao Qiang, a research scientist from UC Berkeley, speaking expressively with an accent in a well-lit room. He describes a project called "Sharp Eyes," which involves "putting camera on every major street and corner of every village in China, meaning everywhere." He continues, explaining how this system matches with "the most advanced artificial intelligence algorithm" to "use this data, real-time data to pick up a face or pick up an action." A distinct, somewhat dramatic, electronic sound effect plays before the video cuts to a tech exhibition. Here, a white surveillance camera with 'Face++' branding is shown, followed by a large screen displaying AI-powered facial recognition software identifying attendees in real-time with green boxes and Chinese text. The narrator explains that "Frequent security expos feature companies like Megvii and its facial recognition technology," showcasing "cameras with AI that can track cars and identify individuals by face, or just by the way they walk." The final shot shows a live street surveillance feed with AI overlays identifying individuals by gender, clothing, and movement with Chinese labels such as '男' (male) and '短发' (short hair).

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a large digital screen displaying a facial recognition interface, where individuals are scanned and identified with overlaid data such as age, gender, and clothing details, including a woman in a beige coat and a man in a black puffer jacket. A male voice explains how these screens track and identify people, noting the incredible proliferation of such technology in just a few years, largely driven by government spending. The scene transitions to a wider view of a tech exhibition, showing multiple screens with real-time AI-driven object detection, including vehicles and pedestrians labeled with Chinese text. The camera then cuts to an interview with Xiao Qiang, a research scientist from UC Berkeley, who states that "China is on its way to building a total surveillance state." Another male voice then identifies the "far northwest of China," the autonomous region of Xinjiang, as "the test lab for the surveillance state." This is followed by a large statue of Mao Zedong overlooking a cityscape, then a wide shot of a military-style parade with armored vehicles and buses. The voice continues, explaining that almost half of Xinjiang's 25 million inhabitants are Muslim Turkic-speaking Uyghurs, as a massive crowd of men, many wearing traditional caps, is shown gathered in a public square. The final frames depict chaotic street scenes with people running and vehicles in motion, including a yellow taxi and a bus, as the voice recounts how tensions in 2009 led to protests and riots in Xinjiang's capital, Urumqi.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with chaotic street scenes, accompanied by sounds of commotion and shouting, showing a group of people in white shirts being pushed or restrained by uniformed personnel. A white armored water cannon vehicle, labeled with red Chinese characters, actively sprays a crowd, indicating a tense confrontation. A male narrator explains that "as the conflict has grown, the authorities have brought in more police and deployed extensive surveillance technology." The scene shifts to a wide shot of a large formation of armed soldiers in green camouflage uniforms and helmets advancing down a street, with a burning vehicle in the foreground, suggesting a state of emergency or conflict. The narrator continues, stating that "that data feeds an AI system that the government claims can predict individuals prone to terrorism and detect those in need of re-education in scores of recently built camps." The setting then changes to a peaceful mosque with traditional Islamic architecture, featuring domes and minarets, where civilians walk freely, and police officers in blue uniforms conduct security checks at the entrance, creating a contrast with the earlier unrest. Inside a classroom, a a teacher raises a large red Chinese national flag with yellow stars as students sit at desks, emphasizing national identity. The video then transitions to a desolate, fenced compound in a barren landscape, possibly a detention facility with surveillance towers and high walls, visually reinforcing the narrator's mention of "recently built camps." The final segment features Sophie Richardson, China Director at Human Rights Watch, speaking in an interview setting with warm, soft lighting, her short gray hair and thin-framed glasses visible, and the bold white 3D FRONTLINE logo displayed in the lower-left corner. She states, "Chinese authorities are without any legal basis arbitrarily detaining up to a million Turkic Muslims simply on the basis of their identity. But even outside the facilities in which these people are being held, most of the population there is being subjected to extraordinary levels of high-tech surveillance, such that almost no aspect of life anymore takes place..."

The scene shifts to a wide shot of a large formation of armed soldiers in camouflage uniforms advancing down a street, with a burning vehicle in the foreground, suggesting a state of emergency or conflict. The setting then changes to a peaceful mosque with traditional Islamic architecture, where civilians walk freely, and police officers in blue uniforms conduct security checks at the entrance, creating a contrast with the earlier unrest. Inside a classroom, a teacher raises a large Chinese national flag as students sit at desks, emphasizing national identity. The video then transitions to a desolate, fenced compound in a barren landscape, possibly a detention facility, with surveillance towers and high walls. audio_updated_entities.Sophie Richardson: Woman with short gray hair, wearing thin-framed glasses, a teal top, and a black cardigan, speaking in an interview setting with warm, soft lighting and the FRONTLINE logo displayed in the lower-left corner, identified as 'China Director, Human Rights Watch'. Her voice is clear and articulate as she discusses the arbitrary detention of up to a million Turkic Muslims and the widespread high-tech surveillance they face.
audio_updated_entities.Narrator: A male voice, calm and authoritative, providing contextual information about the conflict's escalation, the deployment of police and extensive surveillance technology, and the use of AI systems to predict terrorism and identify individuals for 're-education' in newly built camps.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Sophie Richardson, China Director at Human Rights Watch, seated in a dimly lit, elegant room, speaking with expressive hand gestures. Her clear, articulate voice explains how private behaviors like language spoken at home, communication with relatives abroad, and frequency of prayer are being "hoovered up" to decide who is subjected to political re-education in camps. An ominous, low background drone underscores the gravity as the scene transitions to an exterior shot of a high-security detention facility with barbed wire and a Chinese flag atop a tower. A serious male voice then reports "torture and deaths in the camps," adding that for Uyghurs outside, Xinjiang is an "open-air prison." The video cuts to a street scene with police officers in black tactical 'POLICE' and 'SWAT' gear monitoring a public area, while surveillance footage overlays show faces being scanned with white boxes and Chinese text, indicating facial recognition technology in action. A man in a suit, with gray hair and glasses, is then seen speaking into microphones, his urgent, accented voice describing the impossibility of a normal life for Uyghurs, detailing how police force them to scan IDs, take photos, and run data scans on their phones. This intense surveillance is followed by a protest scene where a crowd of demonstrators holds blue flags and signs, including one depicting police violence, highlighting themes of state control and civil resistance, all under the watchful eye of a WSJ logo.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with Sophie Richardson, China Director at Human Rights Watch, seated in a dimly lit, elegant room with soft lighting and a blurred background of furniture and a fireplace. She wears thin-framed glasses, a teal top, a black cardigan, and a silver necklace, speaking with expressive hand gestures. The scene transitions to an exterior shot of a high-security detention facility with barbed wire and a Chinese flag atop a tower, followed by a street scene with police officers in black tactical gear labeled 'POLICE' and 'SWAT' monitoring a public area. Surveillance footage overlays show faces being scanned with white boxes and Chinese text, indicating facial recognition technology in action. The video then cuts to a protest scene with a man in a suit speaking into microphones, followed by a crowd of demonstrators holding blue flags and signs, including one with images of police violence. The atmosphere shifts from formal interview to intense surveillance and public unrest, highlighting themes of state control and civil resistance.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Nury Turkel, a lawyer and prominent Uyghur activist, passionately speaking into a microphone at an outdoor rally, his expression intense as he warns about the installation of "compulsory software, allowing your phone calls and messages to be monitored." The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the demonstration in Washington D.C., where he stands on a stage before a large crowd holding blue and American flags, with banners reading 'Free Our Families' and 'Over Million of Uyghurs Disappeared...'. Protesters hold signs with images of missing family members and messages like 'RELEASE MY SISTER' and 'WHERE IS JUSTICE?', highlighting the narrator's point that many among the Uyghur diaspora have lost all contact with their families back home. The scene shifts to a man scanning a QR code on a wall with his phone, suggesting a digital connection to the protest or missing persons database. The narrative then cuts to an indoor interview with Nury Turkel, identified by on-screen text as from the Uyghur Human Rights Project, wearing a dark suit and red polka-dot tie. With the FRONTLINE logo appearing in the lower-left corner, Turkel seriously explains that this "dystopian deployment of new technology" is a "demonstration project for authoritarian regimes around the world." He elaborates on the use of "barcodes in somebody's home doors to identify what kind of citizen that he is," describing it as "collective punishment of an ethnic group." He further warns that the Chinese government has been "promoting its methods, its technology... to other countries, namely Pakistan, Venezuela, Sudan, and others." The softly lit room with neutral tones creates a formal and somber atmosphere, emphasizing the gravity of his testimony.

The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the rally, where he stands on a stage before a large crowd holding blue flags and American flags, with banners reading 'Free Our Families' and 'Over Million of Uyghurs Disappeared...'. Protesters hold signs with images of missing family members and messages like 'RELEASE MY SISTER' and 'WHERE IS JUSTICE?'. The scene shifts to a man scanning a QR code on a wall with his phone, then cuts to an indoor interview with Nury Turkel, identified by on-screen text as from the Uyghur Human Rights Project, wearing a dark suit and red polka-dot tie, speaking seriously to the camera. The FRONTLINE logo appears in the lower-left corner during his interview, indicating the documentary source. The setting is a softly lit room with neutral-toned walls and furniture, creating a formal and somber atmosphere. The narrative moves from public protest to personal testimony, emphasizing the human cost of the Uyghur crisis.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with Nury Turkel, a man with dark hair in a dark suit, white shirt, and red polka-dot tie, speaking seriously in an indoor interview setting with the FRONTLINE logo visible. He articulates clearly about utilizing something "to squelch political resentment or prevent a political upheaval in their various societies." As a distinct, slightly ominous electronic background music begins, the scene transitions to an aerial view of a long freight train, with blue and red locomotives, moving across a vast, arid desert landscape with mountains and a dry riverbed, emphasizing isolation. A male narrator with an authoritative voice explains that "China has a grand scheme to spread its technology and influence around the world," launched in 2013 along the old Silk Road out of Xinjiang, now going "far beyond," called "the Belt and Road Initiative." During this narration, the visuals shift to a wide shot of a massive concrete dam with water flowing through its spillways, then to a large industrial construction site with steel frameworks, cranes, and heavy machinery, and briefly to a colorful, ornate temple or shrine with intricate carvings. The video then cuts to Paul Mozur, a bearded man in a dark blue shirt, speaking in an office environment with blurred computer monitors, identified by on-screen text as 'PAUL MOZUR, The New York Times'. He elaborates that the Belt and Road is "China's attempt to via spending and investment, project its influence all over the world," citing "massive infrastructure projects" in places like Pakistan, Venezuela, Ecuador, Bolivia, Argentina, and Africa, aiming to "create a political leverage for the Chinese spending campaign all over the globe." As he speaks, the final scene shows Xi Jinping, in a dark suit, walking on a red carpet flanked by a ceremonial guard in red uniforms, shaking hands with another man in a formal outdoor setting with palm trees and greenery, illustrating the global reach of China's influence. The background music continues throughout the narration and Paul Mozur's explanation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a formal handshake between Xi Jinping and a Senegalese official, both in dark suits, against a backdrop of palm trees, marking Xi Jinping's 2018 visit to Senegal, where Chinese contractors had just built a new stadium. The scene transitions to the exterior of the 'ARENE NATIONALE DE LUTTE', a modern stadium with a curved roof, where crowds gather under China and Senegal flags. Inside, Xi Jinping and the Senegalese president walk on a red carpet, waving to a cheering crowd, before being seated formally with Peng Liyuan and a traditional-dressed official on white couches under a banner dated 'July 22, 2018'. The narrative then shifts to a bustling city street in Ecuador, showing the JW Marriott hotel and traffic, as the audio explains how China provides loans for new infrastructure development and offers help maintaining social stability, often leading to Chinese technology being sold and built out in these countries. A close-up of a police officer in a yellow vest at a control center precedes a view of a large surveillance control room, labeled 'ECU-911 SERVICIO INTEGRADO DE SEGURIDAD', where multiple operators in yellow vests monitor numerous live feeds on computer screens and a large wall display. The audio details that these are lower-level camera-based systems, part of a $300 million package to build a ton of cameras and a main center where police can watch them, a trend seen globally in places like Cambodia, Ecuador, and Venezuela. The final shot is a close-up of a white surveillance camera mounted on a pole, underscoring the pervasive monitoring system.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a bustling surveillance control room, likely part of Ecuador’s ECU 911 integrated security service, where multiple operators in yellow vests are seated at desks equipped with multiple monitors displaying live feeds. The room features a large wall of screens showing various city locations, with one monitor prominently displaying the 'ECU 911' logo and the tagline '¡línea única para emergencias!'. The wall of screens also shows 'ECU-911 SERVICIO INTEGRADO DE SEGURIDAD' and specific locations like 'PLAZA FOCH'. The scene then cuts to a close-up interview with Amy Webb, identified as being from the Future Today Institute, who, wearing black-rimmed glasses and a black leather jacket over a patterned blouse, speaks expressively with hand gestures in a softly lit indoor setting. She articulately states, "There are 58 countries that are starting to plug in to China's vision of artificial intelligence... China is in the process of raising a bamboo curtain... one that is sort of all-encompassing, that has shared resources, shared telecommunication systems, shared infrastructure, shared digital systems, even shared mobile phone technologies... that is quickly going up all around the world to the exclusion of us in the West." The video then transitions to Nicholas Thompson, Editor-in-Chief of Wired, who, in a gray suit over a white shirt and speaking directly to the camera with a serious expression and occasional hand gestures in a dimly lit setting, shares his concern: "Well, one of the things I worry about the most is that the world is going to split in two. And that there will be a Chinese tech sector, and there will be an American tech sector. And countries will effectively get to choose which one they want. We kind of like the Cold War, where you decide, oh, are we going to align with the Soviet Union, are we going to align with the United States?" The FRONTLINE logo is visible in the lower-left corner during both interviews.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a man in a gray suit and white shirt speaking in a dimly lit indoor setting, stating, "third world gets to choose this or that. And that's not a world that's good for anybody." The scene transitions to a brightly lit Fox Business Network studio where two female anchors, one in a red patterned dress and the other in a bright pink top, discuss a breaking news story. An on-screen graphic reads 'BREAKING THIS MORNING' and 'HUAWEI CFO ARRESTED,' as one anchor reports, "The markets in Asia and the US falling sharply on news that a top Chinese executive has been arrested in Canada. Her name is Sabrina Meng. She's the CFO of the Chinese telecom Huawei." A nighttime outdoor scene then shows the woman in a dark coat, identified as Sabrina Meng, being escorted by law enforcement officers in high-visibility yellow jackets, one with a Canadian flag patch, as she is led into a black vehicle. A male narrator explains, "News of the dramatic arrest of an important Huawei executive was ostensibly about the company doing business with Iran. But it seemed to be more about American distrust of the company's technology." The video then shows an aerial view of a European-style campus with red-roofed buildings, a river, and greenery, followed by a train crossing a bridge, and people walking up stone steps in a European-style town, illustrating Huawei's headquarters. A laboratory scene with individuals in white lab coats working with equipment and a large screen is also shown, as the narrator describes Huawei as "the second biggest seller of smartphones and the world leader in building 5G networks, the high-speed backbone for the age of AI." A group, including a man in a blue blazer, walks down a grand red-carpeted staircase. The final shot is a close-up of this man, identified by on-screen text as 'REN ZHENGFEI, Founder & CEO, Huawei,' speaking in an ornate interior. The narrator states, "Huawei CEO, a former officer in the People's Liberation Army, was defiant about the American actions," followed by Ren Zhengfei speaking in a non-English language.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Orville Schell, an older man with white hair and glasses, wearing a dark cable-knit sweater over a collared shirt, speaking thoughtfully and reflectively in a dimly lit indoor setting with a golden statue in the background. He discusses the challenge with a company like Huawei, noting that while its head, Ren Zhengfei, claims it's a private entity not taking orders from the Communist Party, intelligence agencies like the Pentagon, National Intelligence Council, and FBI perceive a different reality, anticipating their response when "the call comes." As tense, investigative background music begins, the scene transitions to an outdoor urban environment, showing a close-up of a white surveillance camera mounted on a metal pole with the 'WSJ' logo, followed by multiple dome and box-style surveillance cameras against a modern building with blue and white architectural details. A narrator then explains that the US Commerce Department has blacklisted eight companies for doing business with government agencies in Xinjiang, claiming they aid in the repression of the Muslim minority. This narration continues over a digital interface displaying a live facial recognition system with a grid overlay on a woman’s face and a list of identified individuals in Chinese, and a man interacting with a facial recognition kiosk at a security checkpoint while a uniformed officer observes. A large digital screen then shows a live surveillance feed highlighting a woman’s face with potential matches. The video moves to the interior of a modern office building, where a large yellow robotic arm is displayed in a glass-enclosed area with the 'megvii' logo visible on the wall. The narrator identifies "Megvii" as one of the blacklisted companies, stating they have strongly objected to the blacklist, calling it a "misunderstanding of our company and our technology." A man in a plaid shirt walks past the robotic arm, and the camera pans to an open-plan office space filled with employees working at desks, before concluding with a large screen displaying a live facial recognition test, scanning and identifying a person's face as a man in a gray shirt walks by.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of Tiananmen Square, where a soldier in a dark military uniform stands at attention on a red platform in front of Mao Zedong's iconic portrait, flanked by red banners with Chinese characters. A subtle, almost ominous background soundscape with a distinct whirring sound begins, as a male voiceover states, 'President Xi has increased his authoritarian grip on the country.' The scene transitions to the interior of a grand assembly hall, likely the Great Hall of the People, where rows of delegates in formal suits are seated at long wooden desks, some clapping and others reviewing documents. The camera zooms in on Xi Jinping, seated among the delegates and engaged in conversation, as the voiceover continues, explaining that in 2018, he 'had the Chinese constitution changed so that he could be president for life.' A close-up of the golden National Emblem of the People's Republic of China, with its red background and five stars, mounted on a stone facade, reinforces the nationalistic tone. The video then shifts to a dimly lit indoor setting where a man in a gray suit and white shirt speaks directly to the camera with expressive facial movements, his background blurred with soft lighting. His voice, now clearly the primary audio, reflects on past predictions: 'If you had asked me 20 years ago what would happen to China? I would have said, well, over time, Great Firewall will break down... Eventually, it'll become a much more democratic place, with free expression and lots of Western values.' He then emphatically states, 'And the last time I checked, that has not happened. In fact, technology has become a tool of control.' The final shot returns to a wide-angle view of the massive assembly chamber, filled with delegates, with a large banner reading '中华人民共和国第十三届全国人民代表大会第一次会议' (The First Session of the 13th National People's Congress of the People's Republic of China) above the stage, and numerous journalists with cameras capturing the formal and ceremonial event. The speaker concludes, 'And it may turn out that in fact, technology is a better tool for authoritarian government.'

He explains that if nations are heading into a world of antagonism and conflict, technology becomes something one doesn't want to share, but rather wants to sequester to protect national interests. As he speaks, the scene cuts to a digital surveillance interface displaying a busy city intersection, with vehicles and pedestrians labeled in Chinese text like '非机动车' (non-motorized vehicle) and '行人' (pedestrian), all highlighted with colored bounding boxes and real-time data including '性别' (gender) and '年龄' (age). A close-up shows a '行人抓拍' (pedestrian capture) section with a timestamp and a small video feed of a person in a red jacket, visually reinforcing the technological control he discusses. The video returns to Orville, who continues his commentary, noting that the current 'tipping point' moment, involving artificial intelligence and technological innovation, places us in a completely different framework. He specifically highlights that if China and the US are fundamentally antagonistic, we are in 'a completely different world.' The segment concludes with a nighttime shot of a silhouetted police officer standing before a brightly lit traditional Chinese building, likely Tiananmen Gate, with festive lights and a crowd, creating a stark visual contrast to the intimate interview while underscoring the themes of national interest and surveillance, accompanied by a subtle, almost ominous background music that began during Orville's discussion of the 'tipping point'.

The setting moves indoors to a well-lit event space where Kai-Fu Lee signs copies of his book for an enthusiastic crowd, including a woman in a pink blouse and a young man in a denim shirt with a backpack. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a Go board as a hand places a black stone, setting the stage for a high-stakes game. Yoshua Bengio, a man with gray hair and a beard wearing a red collared shirt, speaks directly to the camera against a whiteboard with faint mathematical diagrams, stating that while AI can be beneficial, its current use isn't necessarily aligned with building a better society, but "we could change that." Dramatic, slightly ominous orchestral music begins as a calm, authoritative male narrator introduces the Google DeepMind Challenge Match from March 8–15, 2016, as a glimpse into the future of artificial intelligence, emphasizing the need for a careful strategy to harness this "new and awesome power." The scene transitions to the formal, blue-lit match room where Lee Se-dol, a South Korean Go grandmaster in a dark suit, is seated across from the AlphaGo team, represented by individuals in suits behind a blue partition labeled 'AlphaGo TEAM'. Monitors display the game and timers as Lee Se-dol makes moves with deliberate precision, deep in concentration, while the AlphaGo team observes intently. The video cuts back to Yoshua Bengio, who continues, expressing concern that "democracy is threatened by the progress of these tools unless we improve our social norms and we increase the collective wisdom at the planet level to deal with that increased power," adding that he hopes his concerns are "not founded, but the stakes are so high." The scene returns to the match, capturing Lee Se-dol contemplating his next move, hand on chin, then placing a stone with focus, highlighting the tension and intellectual rigor of this human-AI confrontation, all underscored by the persistent dramatic music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man with gray hair and a beard, wearing a red collared shirt, speaking directly to the camera in an interview setting, gesturing expressively as he explains a technical concept and writes equations on a transparent surface with a blue marker, stating, "I don't think we should take these concerns lightly. I don't think we can play with those possibilities and just... race ahead without thinking about the potential outcomes." The scene transitions to a close-up of a hand placing a white pill on a wooden surface with a distinct "clink" sound, followed by a black screen and a sustained, low, ominous synth sound. The video then shifts to a dynamic montage of AI and automation, framed within a digital interface with the URL 'pbs.org/frontline' displayed at the bottom, as a male voiceover begins, "Go to pbs.org/frontline for more of the impact of AI on jobs." The montage includes a factory with yellow robotic arms assembling vehicles, a white semi-truck named 'Betsy Mae' driving on a desert highway with augmented reality overlays, and an empty driverless truck cab. Another male voice adds, "I believe about 50% of jobs will be somewhat or extremely threatened by AI in the next 15 years or so," as a robotic arm works on a car. The voiceover continues, "And a look at the potential for racial bias in this technology," while a facial recognition system analyzes a person with digital overlays and Chinese text. A woman in a black top, seen in an interview, states, "We've had issues with bias, with discrimination, with poor system design." The sequence continues with social media graphics showing the FRONTLINE Twitter and Facebook pages, highlighting engagement metrics and content about AI’s impact on jobs and racial bias, as the voiceover encourages viewers to "Connect to the Frontline community on Facebook and Twitter and watch anytime on the PBS Video app or pbs.org/frontline." The final frames display the FRONTLINE PBS Video App on a smartphone, followed by funding credits for FRONTLINE, including the MacArthur Foundation, with a dark, tech-themed background and blue light streaks, all accompanied by dramatic orchestral music and digital sound effects.

He gestures expressively as he explains a technical concept, writing equations on a transparent surface with a blue marker. The scene transitions to a close-up of a hand placing a white pill on a wooden surface, followed by a black screen. The video then shifts to a dynamic montage of AI and automation, framed within a digital interface with the URL 'pbs.org/frontline' displayed at the bottom. The montage includes a factory with robotic arms assembling vehicles, a semi-truck driving on a desert highway with augmented reality overlays, a driverless truck cab, a robotic arm working on a car, a facial recognition system analyzing a person, and a woman speaking in an interview. The sequence continues with social media graphics showing the FRONTLINE Twitter and Facebook pages, highlighting engagement metrics and content about AI’s impact on jobs and racial bias. The final frames display the FRONTLINE PBS Video App on a smartphone, followed by funding credits for FRONTLINE, including the MacArthur Foundation, with a dark, tech-themed background and blue light streaks.

segments.integrated_caption: The video transitions into the closing credit sequence for the FRONTLINE documentary 'In the Age of AI', set against a dark, abstract digital background with faint data streams and light flares, creating a high-tech, cinematic atmosphere. A dramatic, slightly melancholic orchestral score with electronic elements plays throughout. The FRONTLINE logo, bold white on red, is fixed in the upper-left as credits for directors, writers, producers, editors, and crew, including Neil Docherty, David Fanning, and Michele Hozer, roll vertically in clean white text. A male voice states, "For more on this and other Frontline programs, visit our website at pbs.org/frontline," as the URL 'pbs.org/frontline' appears on the right. Funding acknowledgments follow, displaying logos and text for the MacArthur Foundation, Ford Foundation, Abrams Foundation, Park Foundation, FRONTLINE Journalism Fund, and Corporation for Public Broadcasting. Concurrently, a DVD cover for 'In the Age of AI', featuring a blue and purple design with a human face overlaid with binary code, is shown on the right, priced at $24.99 plus S&H. The male voice continues, "To order Frontline's In the Age of AI on DVD, visit shopPBS.org or call 1-800-PLAY-PBS. This program is also available on Amazon Prime Video," directly referencing the visual. The final credits list executive producers and legal information, concluding with a copyright notice for © 2017 Dow Jones & Company Inc., as the music continues to play, maintaining a professional and polished tone.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dark, digital-themed credit sequence for FRONTLINE, accompanied by a dramatic, orchestral fanfare that immediately establishes a sense of anticipation and importance. As red and blue lens flares sweep over a blurred background of data streams and computer interfaces, the music, featuring prominent brass instruments like trumpets and trombones, alongside strings and percussion including snare drums and timpani, builds in intensity. The bold white 'FRONTLINE' logo appears on a red rectangle in the upper-left corner, followed by the executive producer's name, Raney Aronson-Rath. The sequence transitions to display 'A FRONTLINE production with Five O’Clock Films' in white text against a red horizontal light streak, while the powerful, anticipatory fanfare continues. Copyright information, '© 2019 WGBH Educational Foundation ALL RIGHTS RESERVED' and 'FRONTLINE is a production of WGBH/Boston, which is solely responsible for its content,' is then shown against the same dark, data-filled backdrop. The orchestral fanfare reaches a distinct crescendo, characteristic of a news program or documentary opening, just as a bright, fiery orange and yellow animation of the 'WGBH' logo appears, glowing and pulsing with light. The music abruptly cuts off as the WGBH logo fades to black, concluding the sleek, high-tech, and professional opening.

The overall atmosphere is sleek, high-tech, and professional, emphasizing the documentary's production quality and institutional backing.

entity_library.FRONTLINE Logo: Bold white 3D text on a red background, appearing in the lower-left corner during the interview scene, indicating the documentary source.
entity_library.Server Room: Long corridor of black server racks with red indicator lights, creating a high-tech ambiance, as a male voiceover declares, 'Tonight. The race to become an AI superpower is on.'
entity_library.Man in Suit (Serious Expression): Man with dark, wavy hair, wearing a gray suit over a white shirt, speaking thoughtfully about how algorithms are changing human behavior and how WIRED has shifted from a champion to an observer of technological change, all in a dimly lit indoor setting with soft, blurred background lighting.
entity_library.Facial Recognition Interface: Digital overlay on a large screen showing individuals being scanned with grid patterns and identification data, with Chinese text, appearing as a male voiceover discusses 'racial bias in this technology.'
entity_library.Formal Assembly Scene: A man in a dark suit standing and being applauded by others in a large hall with red seats and wooden desks, with Chinese banners visible, suggesting a political or state event, accompanied by dramatic background music.
entity_library.Yellow Robotic Arm: Large yellow industrial robot arm in a factory setting, actively moving with visible mechanical joints and safety markings, labeled 'FANUC', assembling car chassis and handling components with precision. These are part of 'one of the world's largest builders of industrial robots,' a 'Japanese-owned company called Fanuc Robotics,' which has been producing robots for 'well over 35 years' and is now 'utilizing artificial intelligence' to make them 'easier to use' and handle a 'broader spectrum of opportunities.'
entity_library.Semi-Truck 'Betsy Mae': White semi-truck with 'Betsy Mae' written on the cab, operated by 'Mid Seven Transportation', featuring orange and blue stripes, a blue ribbon decal, and elevated on a red hydraulic lift inside a dimly lit garage, which is the central subject of the conversation regarding the feasibility and impact of self-driving technology on the trucking industry.
entity_library.Ocado Warehouse Robots: Multiple white, boxy autonomous robots with yellow and blue tops, labeled with identifiers like 'AA-C39' and 'AA-F71', moving along grid paths in a large, well-lit warehouse, carrying yellow storage pods. These robots are described by a narrator as being 'guided by AI' to 'pack everything from soap powder to cantaloupes for online consumers' and are 'machines that pick groceries'.
entity_library.Man in Suit (Speaking): Wearing a gray suit and white shirt, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit indoor setting with a blurred background. His voice is clear and reflective, expressing surprise that China has not become more democratic as predicted, and noting that technology has become a tool of control for authoritarian government.
entity_library.Facebook Interface: Computer screen displaying a 'Being Patriotic' sponsored page, shown as a voiceover encourages viewers to 'Connect to the Frontline community on Facebook and Twitter.'
entity_library.Google Building: Aerial view of a modern glass building with 'Google' written in large white letters and solar panels on the roof, while a female expert continues to discuss Google's surveillance.
entity_library.Surveillance Camera: White surveillance camera mounted on a metal pole, shown in close-up, representing the lower-level camera-based systems that are part of a $300 million package to build a ton of cameras for emergency response, enabling pervasive monitoring.
entity_library.LG CLOi Robot: White, rounded robot with a black circular face and glowing blue eyes, labeled 'LG CLOi', positioned in a dimly lit indoor environment.
entity_library.Facial Recognition Smartphone Interface: Smartphone screen displaying a live video feed with a digital overlay for facial recognition, including a blue button and identification data, highlighted by Wu Wenhao as having a speed of less than 100 milliseconds (0.1 second) on mobile devices.
entity_library.Go Game Scene: Two men seated at a wooden table playing Go in a dimly lit, traditional-style room; one man in a dark suit with his back to the camera, the other in a blue shirt reaching for a stone from a wooden box. The scene is accompanied by a male narrator explaining that Emperor Yao devised Go to teach his son discipline, concentration, and balance, a theme that resonates in the age of AI.
entity_library.Funding Credit Sequence: Series of text and logos on a dark background, including MacArthur Foundation, Ford Foundation, Abrams Foundation, Park Foundation, FRONTLINE Journalism Fund, and Corporation for Public Broadcasting, with text thanking PBS viewers and acknowledging major supporters, accompanied by dramatic orchestral music and digital sound effects.
entity_library.Challenge Match Event: A formal event with a stage backdrop displaying 'Challenge Match 8–15 March 2016' and network diagrams, where the Go world champion takes on an AI program developed by Google, described by a female reporter as a 'breakthrough moment'.
entity_library.Outdoor City Screen: A large digital billboard in a busy city street displaying a live Go game and a portrait of a man, with Korean text and traffic passing by, indicating public engagement with the event.
entity_library.Media Crew: A group of journalists and camera operators surrounding a man in a suit, capturing the event with professional cameras and microphones, emphasizing the media attention.
entity_library.Pedro Domingos: Man with gray hair, wearing a dark suit and white shirt, speaking in an interview setting with the FRONTLINE logo and his affiliation (Univ. of Washington) displayed on screen; he is shown writing equations on a whiteboard and then speaking directly to the camera with animated gestures, explaining how computers personalize and adapt the world to us, but warning of the danger that companies controlling algorithms may not share our goals, urging awareness for control.
entity_library.Lee Se-dol: South Korean Go grandmaster, wearing a dark suit, seen playing Go with intense focus, reacting to moves with contemplative gestures, and seated at a wooden board with a monitor displaying the game.
entity_library.AlphaGo Team: Group of individuals in suits seated behind a blue partition, observing the Go match, with 'AlphaGo TEAM' printed on the back of their chairs.
entity_library.Google DeepMind Challenge Match Screen: Large projection screen displaying countdowns, match details ('Google DeepMind Challenge Match 8–15 March 2016'), and real-time game data during the Go match, including timers for Lee Se-dol and AlphaGo, accompanied by a commentator's exclamation in Korean and English, 'Oh, totally unthinkable move,' and a voiceover announcing 'Game two. Move 37.'
entity_library.Public TV Display Wall: Multiple television screens in a store, all showing the same live Go match broadcast, with Korean text overlays and a news ticker announcing Lee Se-dol’s defeat.
entity_library.Nicholas Thompson: Man with gray hair and glasses, wearing a gray suit over a white shirt, speaking directly to the camera with a serious expression and occasional hand gestures in a dimly lit indoor setting, expressing concerns about the world splitting into Chinese and American tech sectors, with a clear, thoughtful voice.
entity_library.Kai-Fu Lee: Man with dark hair and glasses, wearing a blue suit, light blue shirt, and a patterned tie, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit setting with a softly blurred background, his expression earnest and engaged. He articulates the ethical dilemma of using AI for citizen safety without encroaching on individual rights and privacy, noting that every country will be tempted to push AI beyond acceptable limits. His voice is clear and articulate, and he is later identified by a narrator as an architect of AI.
entity_library.U.S. Capitol Building: Nighttime view of the U.S. Capitol with illuminated windows and an American flag waving, symbolizing governmental authority, visually connected by a narrator's voice discussing lobbyists in Washington seeking privacy standard changes.
entity_library.Baidu World 2018 Event: High-tech exhibition environment with glowing displays, including a screen showing '2018 BAIDU' and a large audience in a dimly lit auditorium under blue lighting, aurally contextualized by a male voice discussing Baidu's role in China's AI ambitions and challenges with American technology, accompanied by faint crowd sounds.
entity_library.Clinton Avenue Street Sign: Green street sign reading 'CLINTON AV' visible above a person riding a scooter in an outdoor urban setting, appearing as the narrator introduces the theme of AI changing the world.
entity_library.China World Tower: Illuminated skyscraper with '中国国贸 China World' signage, visible in a night-time cityscape, symbolizing modern Chinese urban development, appearing as the narrator declares, 'China has decided to chase the AI future'.
entity_library.Baidu World 2018 Stage: Presentation stage with dynamic blue light patterns and a speaker in a white shirt and dark pants, engaging an audience in a futuristic, tech-focused environment, as a voice (likely Kai-Fu Lee) discusses the 'internet mindset and the AI mindset'.
entity_library.Vintage Archival Footage: Colorized historical scene from the 1980s featuring a woman in a lavender blazer and black turtleneck, smiling warmly, followed by an old Apple computer screen with the text 'Good Morning America! And good morning, Joan.' in a classic serif font, from which a computerized voice is heard demonstrating speech recognition technology with phrases like "Casper, copy this to make right too."
entity_library.Woman in White Shirt (Tech Event): Woman in a white shirt holding a smartphone, interacting with a digital security verification interface, her face illuminated by the screen’s glow, standing beside a man in a black t-shirt with a lanyard.
entity_library.Tech Event Attendee (Young Man): Young man in a black hoodie labeled 'AI Empowering Business', standing at a desk with dual monitors displaying code, engaged in conversation with Kai-Fu Lee.
entity_library.Paul Mozur: Man with a beard, wearing a dark blue shirt, speaking in an office environment with computer monitors blurred in the background, identified by on-screen text as 'PAUL MOZUR, The New York Times'. He speaks clearly and informatively about the Belt and Road Initiative being 'China's attempt to via spending and investment, project its influence all over the world,' citing 'massive infrastructure projects' globally and aiming to 'create a political leverage for the Chinese spending campaign all over the globe.'
entity_library.Belt and Road Forum for International Co: Large-scale international conference with a grand hall and thousands of attendees, where, as narrated by an authoritative voice, Xi Jinping announced China's bold new plans to catch up with the US in AI by 2025 and lead the world by 2030, with another speaker discussing 'intensified cooperation in frontier areas such as digital economy'.
entity_library.Risk Assessment Interface: Digital screen displaying a credit or security evaluation system with Chinese text such as '审批不通过' (approval denied), 'WARNING', and progress indicators like '100%', used to assess a man’s eligibility for a loan or service.
entity_library.7FRESH Food Market: Modern grocery store with automated checkout systems, digital price tags, and a self-service payment kiosk where customers scan items and use facial recognition for payment, directly illustrating the narrator's point about shopping in stores without cashiers, where facial recognition is the currency.
entity_library.Warehouse Robots (JD Logistics): Red autonomous robots with numbered labels (e.g., '6350') moving across a grid of yellow docking stations in a large, organized warehouse, part of a JD Logistics system, with Chinese signage indicating '智能' (Intelligence) and '正道成功' (Success through the Right Path), visually representing China's leadership in e-commerce and advanced logistics as described by the narrator.
entity_library.Outdoor Surveillance Setup: White surveillance camera mounted on a stand in an outdoor courtyard, capturing a red box placed on a green mat, suggesting a delivery or monitoring process.
entity_library.Face++ Biometric System: Large digital screen in an office hallway displaying employee faces and names with a blue interface, powered by Face++ technology, used for biometric access control, showcasing the advanced AI implementation and rapid growth of AI 'unicorns' discussed in the audio.
entity_library.Smartphone Payment Interface: A close-up of a white smartphone displaying a financial app with a '确认借款' (Confirm Loan) button, which is discussed by a narrator and JIAO KE as part of an AI-driven loan application process that analyzes user behavior like typing confidence and phone battery charge to assess creditworthiness.
entity_library.Tech Event Attendee (Young Man in Green: Young man in a green jacket, standing beside the young man in the black hoodie, listening attentively as Kai-Fu Lee speaks.
entity_library.Young Man on Phone: Young man in a gray hoodie, identified as Zhao Shudong, the 33-year-old CEO of the startup Momenta, who is heard speaking animatedly in Chinese and English, expressing his belief that autonomous vehicles will 'definitely surpass the human driver in the end' and discussing technical details like 'another cutting'. He is initially seen speaking into a smartphone in a car and later participating in an office meeting about autonomous driving technology.
entity_library.Modern Office Workspace: Open-plan office with white arches, glass partitions, wooden railings, and a staircase, featuring a man in a dark suit at a desk, and later two men seated at a round wooden table in a bright, airy space with large windows and a potted plant, engaged in a professional conversation, over which a narrator discusses the global impact of the AI automation research findings.
entity_library.Lincoln Sedan: White Lincoln sedan with license plate '京Q·70VC8', driving on a highway, equipped with autonomous driving technology, shown from front and side angles, while a narrator mentions Mercedes-Benz as an early investor.
entity_library.Woman in Black Top: A woman with dark hair, wearing a black top, seen in an interview setting, stating, 'We've had issues with bias, with discrimination, with poor system design.'
entity_library.Driver in Dark Jacket: Older man with a gray beard and glasses, wearing a black long-sleeve shirt with a red logo, identified by a narrator as Harry Cripps, an auto worker and local union president, who speaks with concern about his city being left behind in economic recovery.
entity_library.Traffic Jam Pilot Presentation Slide: Large screen displaying a technical slide titled 'Traffic Jam Pilot – Definition – Operational Design Domain (ODD)', with bullet points on driver attention, speed range (0–60 km/h), environmental conditions, and legal compliance, used during the office meeting.
entity_library.Cao Xudong: Young man in a gray hoodie, identified as Founder & CEO of Momenta, speaking thoughtfully in an office setting, his voice expressing excitement and gratitude for living in the current era.
entity_library.Audience at Presentation: Group of attendees seated in a large room, attentively watching a presentation titled 'How Momenta Works', including a man in a plaid shirt and a woman in a black top, as background music plays and a narrator discusses smartphone usage in China.
entity_library.Subway Commuters: People in a subway station, some using smartphones, with one man in a brown jacket and another in a blue jacket walking through turnstiles, while a narrator highlights the prevalence of smartphones and apps like WeChat in China.
entity_library.Digital Interface (Finance/Shopping): Hand interacting with a tablet displaying icons for finance, shopping, and data, with a green box showing '800万' (8 million) and Chinese text, illustrating the widespread use of digital services in China as described by a narrator.
entity_library.7FRESH Food Market Kiosk: Customer at a self-service kiosk, scanning a QR code for payment, with the screen showing a total of 43.5 yuan and payment options via WeChat or Alipay, visually reinforcing the narrator's point about China being the best place for AI implementation.
entity_library.Presentation Screen (How Momenta Works): Large screen displaying the title 'How Momenta Works' in a presentation setting, with a speaker in a gray hoodie standing at a podium.
entity_library.Subway Turnstile: Metal turnstile in a subway station with red lighting, through which commuters pass, indicating automated access control.
entity_library.QR Code Payment Screen: Digital screen at a market stall showing a green QR code with Chinese text, used for mobile payments, visible as people scan it with their smartphones.
entity_library.Delivery Rider (Meituan Waimai): Rider in a yellow uniform with a '美团外卖' (Meituan Waimai) box on a scooter, navigating a city street, indicating food delivery services.
entity_library.Delivery Rider (Dada): Rider in a blue uniform with a '达达' (Dada) box on a scooter, riding through a city street, representing another delivery platform.

entity_library.Self-Service Kiosk Users: People interacting with large touchscreen kiosks in a nighttime urban setting, one screen displaying 'HANDMADE' and another showing a menu, suggesting digital ordering or payment.

entity_library.Market Vendor (Fruit Stand): Person behind a fruit stand with a green QR code sign, facilitating mobile payments for produce.

entity_library.JIAO KE: Founder & CEO of Smart Finance, seen speaking directly to the camera in an office setting, he explains in a calm, professional tone with a slight accent how phone battery levels correlate with loan delinquency rates, noting lower battery indicates higher risk.
entity_library.Employee in Gray and White Striped Shirt: Man seated at a desk, typing on a keyboard, wearing a gray and white horizontally striped long-sleeve shirt, with a brown bag on his lap.
entity_library.Employee in Blue Sweater: Man sitting beside the striped-shirt employee, wearing a blue sweater over a white collared shirt, looking at a monitor with a spreadsheet open.
entity_library.Employee with Sunglasses: Man wearing dark sunglasses and a black shirt, seated at a desk with a monitor displaying code, seen from behind in a side view.
entity_library.Employee with Code Monitor: Person working at a desk with a large monitor showing lines of code, with a plush toy and various desk items nearby.
entity_library.Smart Finance Office: Modern office with glass partitions, white desks, and orange cubicle dividers, where employees are working on computers, including a woman in a plaid dress and glasses, and a man in a gray shirt.
entity_library.Neural Network Visualization: Blue-toned digital display showing glowing neurons and interconnected data streams, symbolizing the underlying AI technology.
entity_library.Wu Wenhao: Vice President of Megvii, wearing a black sweater over a white collared shirt, speaking directly to the camera in an office setting with blurred digital screens in the background, detailing the facial recognition speed on mobile devices (less than 100ms) and asserting Megvii's pioneering role in bringing facial recognition to 'commercial quality'.
entity_library.Woman in White Blouse (Restaurant): Woman with shoulder-length brown hair, wearing a white blouse and star-shaped earrings, interacting with a self-service kiosk in a modern restaurant setting, her actions implicitly linked by the narrator to the ability to 'pay for your KFC with a smile' using facial recognition.
entity_library.Restaurant Staff Member: Man wearing a white shirt and green apron, standing behind a wooden counter, handing a small green item to the customer during a transaction.
entity_library.Xiao Qiang: Research scientist from UC Berkeley, wearing a gray blazer over a black shirt, speaking with expressive hand gestures in a well-lit indoor setting with white doors and windows in the background, who states, 'China is on its way to building a total surveillance state.'
